title: Dashboard Type Accuracy & Deletion UX
summary: This release fixes two dashboard-level issues: an incorrect TypeScript type on the Project object that included a field no longer returned by the list API, and a blocking browser dialog used for project deletion that was inconsistent with the dashboard's modal-based interface.

whatsIncluded.headline: Accurate Project Type
whatsIncluded.detail: Removed apiKey from the shared Project interface, which was never included in list endpoint responses after the plain-text key removal. A separate ProjectCreateResponse type now correctly models the one-time key returned only on project creation.

whatsIncluded.headline: Modal-Based Delete Confirmation
whatsIncluded.detail: Replaced the synchronous window.confirm() dialog on project deletion with a styled DeleteConfirmModal component, consistent with all other modal interactions on the dashboard. The modal shows the project name, disables both buttons during deletion, and clears itself on success or cancellation.

title: SDK Code Quality — Shared UUID Generation
summary: This release eliminates a code duplication issue in the Analytics SDK where the UUID generation function was defined independently in two separate modules. The function is now defined once and shared across the SDK.

whatsIncluded.headline: Centralized UUID Generation
whatsIncluded.detail: The generateUUID() function was identically duplicated in both session.ts and visitor.ts. It has been extracted into storage.ts as a single shared export, ensuring both modules always use the same implementation with no risk of the copies diverging.

whatsIncluded.headline: Reduced Bundle Footprint
whatsIncluded.detail: Removing the duplicate implementation slightly reduces the compiled SDK output, as the same logic is no longer emitted twice into the bundle.

whatsIncluded.headline: SDK Bumped to v0.2.10
whatsIncluded.detail: The Runtime SDK is updated to v0.2.10 to reflect this internal code quality improvement.

title: Config Unification & SDK Zero-Config
summary: This update hardens the developer experience by unifying environment variable access across all backend services and simplifies SDK integration by defaulting to active tracking.

whatsIncluded.headline: Unified Environment Configuration
whatsIncluded.detail: Centralized all process.env access into a strictly-typed config object across both standard and scalable backends, eliminating fragmented environment lookups.

whatsIncluded.headline: Coordinated System-wide Bump
whatsIncluded.detail: Synchronized versioning (v1.0.14) across the root and both backend architectures to ensure full configuration compatibility.

title: High-Efficiency Batching & Timezone Sync
summary: This update introduces a consolidated analytics batch endpoint and intelligent timezone bucket alignment, while significantly reducing network bandwidth through global Gzip compression.

whatsIncluded.headline: Batched Analytics Endpoint
whatsIncluded.detail: Consolidated 6 separate parallel analytics calls into a single high-performance request, ensuring faster dashboard loads and reduced request overhead.

whatsIncluded.headline: Universal Timezone Support
whatsIncluded.detail: Added support for user-defined IANA timezones in time-series charts using absolute UTC offset mapping and string-based day extraction, ensuring that daily data buckets align perfectly with local calendars everywhere.

whatsIncluded.headline: Global Response Compression
whatsIncluded.detail: Activated middleware-level Gzip compression across all backend services, drastically reducing the transmission size of JSON analytics data.

title: Performance & Architecture Hygiene
summary: This optimization pass introduces low-latency caching for ownership verification and strengthens data privacy by removing sensitive hashes from the project listing API.

whatsIncluded.headline: Analytics Ownership Caching
whatsIncluded.detail: Added a 5-second TTL cache to both legacy and scalable backends to deduplicate parallel ownership checks, reducing redundant database load during dashboard refreshes.

whatsIncluded.headline: Selective Project Metadata
whatsIncluded.detail: Strengthened API security by ensuring the project list endpoint only returns required metadata (ID, Name, Dates), explicitly omitting internal API key hashes.

whatsIncluded.headline: Cross-Architecture Optimization Sync
whatsIncluded.detail: Ensured both standard and horizontally scalable backend versions are feature-parity with respect to these high-performance optimizations.

title: Legal Transparency & Footer Modals
summary: This update introduces dedicated Privacy Policy and Terms of Service modals, wires up all footer links to their respective modals, and extracts legal content into maintainable data files.

whatsIncluded.headline: Privacy Policy Modal
whatsIncluded.detail: A new dedicated modal covering data collection, visitor and session identity, API key security, data ownership, GDPR compliance, and data retention policies.

whatsIncluded.headline: Terms of Service Modal
whatsIncluded.detail: A new dedicated modal covering acceptable use, account access requirements, API key limits, rate limiting policies, and service availability guarantees.

whatsIncluded.headline: Footer Link Integration
whatsIncluded.detail: The footer Changelog, Privacy, and Terms links now correctly open their respective modals using a lightweight global store, with zero duplication of existing components.

title: API Key Security & Hardening
summary: This update introduces SHA-256 API key hashing, ingest array capping, and metadata sanitization to protect the system at scale.

whatsIncluded.headline: API Key Hashing
whatsIncluded.detail: Upgraded storage to use deterministic SHA-256 hashes for all API keys, ensuring plain-text keys are never stored in the database.

whatsIncluded.headline: Ingest Guardrails
whatsIncluded.detail: Implemented strict capping of 500 events per request and metadata type sanitization to prevent memory flooding and injection attacks.

whatsIncluded.headline: Performance Tuning
whatsIncluded.detail: Optimized the dashboard analytics rate limiter from 15 minutes to 60 seconds (max 300 requests) for a significantly more responsive experience.

title: Security Hardening Update
summary: This critical security update addresses CSRF vulnerabilities and strengthens identity verification and CORS policies across all backend services.

whatsIncluded.headline: Logout CSRF Shield
whatsIncluded.detail: Implemented strict origin validation for the logout process to prevent unauthorized cross-site logout attempts.

whatsIncluded.headline: JWT Production Lockdown
whatsIncluded.detail: Enforced a fatal startup check that prevents the system from running in production with insecure or default secrets.

title: Universal Ingestion Optimization
summary: This update synchronizes high-performance caching and analytics accuracy across both monolithic and scalable backend architectures.

whatsIncluded.headline: Cross-Architecture API Caching
whatsIncluded.detail: Extended in-process LRU caching for API key validation to the Scalable Gateway, removing redundant DB queries and increasing system-wide ingestion throughput.

whatsIncluded.headline: Unified Browser Analytics
whatsIncluded.detail: Standardized the browser grouping logic across all metric engines to ensure consistent dashboard reporting for events without browser metadata.

title: SDK Production Endpoint (v2)
summary: Critical follow-up to coordinate a fresh SDK build (v0.2.7) with the correct production ingestion endpoint fully baked into the distribution.

whatsIncluded.headline: SDK Endpoint Hardening
whatsIncluded.detail: Ensured the build-time ingestion URL is correctly injected into the SDK minified bundle for zero-config production reliability.

title: SDK Memory & Lifecycle Fixes
summary: This release resolves a critical memory leak and redundant monkeypatching issue in the Analytics SDK history tracking.

whatsIncluded.headline: SDK History Leak (B5)
whatsIncluded.detail: Eliminated redundant monkeypatching and memory leaks in the Analytics SDK by implementing a global instance registry and unified history tracking.

title: Honest Types & Code Hygiene
summary: This release resolves all outstanding linting issues and implements proper, non-evasive type safety across the entire architecture.

whatsIncluded.headline: Honest Type Casting
whatsIncluded.detail: Replaced 'any' casts with robust library types, ensuring the codebase is 100% type-safe without needing to suppress linter warnings.

whatsIncluded.headline: Zero-Warning Build
whatsIncluded.detail: Performed a full hygiene pass to remove unused code and resolve every warning, achieving a perfectly clean build for production.

whatsIncluded.headline: Deployment Stability
whatsIncluded.detail: Optimized the deployment pipeline to prioritize clean container lifecycles, reducing potential startup race conditions.

title: Timestamp & Auth Hardening
summary: This update fixes timestamp compression in high-volume ingestion and standardizes JWT security protocols across the architecture.

whatsIncluded.headline: Timestamp Accuracy (B3)
whatsIncluded.detail: Removed hardcoded creation dates from bulk ingestion, ensuring that every event receives a unique, database-native timestamp for better time-series granularity.

whatsIncluded.headline: Unified Security & Architect (v1.0.3)
whatsIncluded.detail: Synchronized environment configuration (env.ts) across all services including the worker, removed redundant .env.example files, and added startup safety assertions.

whatsIncluded.headline: Timestamp Precision
whatsIncluded.detail: Fixed ingestion timestamp compression by using database-native arrival times, restoring accurate granularity to all time-series charts.

title: Security & Architecture Resiliency Patch
summary: This patch release addresses two critical backend edge cases related to global error handling and GitHub authentication flow limits.

whatsIncluded.headline: API Error Hardening
whatsIncluded.detail: Secured the global error middleware to ensure internal database schema errors or stack traces are never leaked to the client during a 500 Internal Server Error in production.

whatsIncluded.headline: OAuth Flow Reliability
whatsIncluded.detail: Adjusted the API rate limiter to specifically target only the initial GitHub authentication redirect, preventing false-positive lockouts from occurring dynamically during the OAuth callback hand-off.

title: SDK Production Transport Fix
summary: Critical update to the Vyzora SDK (v0.2.4) to ensure the default transport endpoint matches the newly deployed production ingestion infrastructure.

whatsIncluded.headline: SDK Endpoint Synchronization
whatsIncluded.detail: Hardcoded the production ingestion URL into the SDK distribution, ensuring zero-config connectivity for out-of-the-box installations.

whatsIncluded.headline: Version Hardening
whatsIncluded.detail: Billed as a patch release (v1.0.1) to synchronize the workspace with the official SDK stable build (v0.2.4).

title: Vyzora v1.0 Production Release
summary: The official v1.0.0 release of Vyzora. This milestone marks the full transition to a high-performance, horizontally scalable microservice architecture designed for enterprise-grade ingestion and analytics.

whatsIncluded.headline: Production-Ready Ingestion
whatsIncluded.detail: The scalable backend is now the default. Includes API pooling, worker batching, and high-concurrency optimizations for PostgreSQL.

whatsIncluded.headline: Infrastructure Stability
whatsIncluded.detail: Verified 100% success rate under high concurrent load. Optimized for Supabase Transaction Mode and multi-replica horizontal scaling.

whatsIncluded.headline: Developer Experience v1.0
whatsIncluded.detail: Simplified orchestration with 'npm run dev:scalable' defaulting to a 3-instance API/Worker cluster. Added 'npm run stress' for instant performance validation.

title: Scalable Microservice Architecture
summary: This update introduces Vyzora's new horizontally scalable infrastructure. We've moved from a monolithic synchronous model to an asynchronous 'Producer-Consumer' pattern, allowing for massive event throughput without slowing down the dashboard.

whatsIncluded.headline: Microservice Separation
whatsIncluded.detail: Split the backend into independent API (Producer), Worker (Consumer), and Nginx (Load Balancer) services. Each component is now independently scalable and containerized.

whatsIncluded.headline: In-Memory Queuing Engine
whatsIncluded.detail: Integrated BullMQ and Redis as a high-performance message bus. Events are now 'written to the rail' instantly, ensuring 200 OK responses in milliseconds while ingestion happens in the background.

whatsIncluded.headline: One-Click Infrastructure
whatsIncluded.detail: Introduced Root-level orchestration (npm run dev:scalable) using Docker Compose to handle complex multi-service networking locally and in production.

title: Feedback System & Mobile Optimization
summary: This update introduces a premium user feedback system and a comprehensive mobile responsiveness pass across the landing page and documentation.

whatsIncluded.headline: User Feedback System
whatsIncluded.detail: Launched a glassmorphism feedback modal integrated with SendGrid. Includes automated email notifications with professional HTML templates and improved deliverability safeguards.

whatsIncluded.headline: Mobile Responsiveness Pass
whatsIncluded.detail: Completely refined the mobile experience: simplified navbar animations, optimized hero section centering, and hardened the documentation layout for small screens.

whatsIncluded.headline: Version Synchronization
whatsIncluded.detail: Frontend bumped to v0.6.2 and Root bumped to v0.9.4 to finalize the feedback and accessibility milestone.

title: CORS Refinement & Universal Reliability
summary: This patch release fixes a critical CORS regression where wildcard origins were blocked for requests involving credentials. We've pivoted to a dynamic reflected-origin strategy to ensure universal compatibility.

whatsIncluded.headline: Reflected Origin Support
whatsIncluded.detail: Replaced the brittle wildcard (*) CORS policy with a dynamic reflected-origin check (origin: true). This allows cross-domain analytics to work even when browsers include credentials in their requests.

whatsIncluded.headline: Backend Stabilization
whatsIncluded.detail: Backend bumped to v0.5.2 and Root bumped to v0.9.3 to finalize the cross-domain ingestion infrastructure.

title: Universal Ingestion & SDK Reliability
summary: This release marks a significant milestone in Vyzora's capability as a cross-domain analytics platform. We've unlocked universal event ingestion and hardened the SDK's tracking reliability for modern web frameworks.

whatsIncluded.headline: Universal Ingestion Endpoint
whatsIncluded.detail: Updated the backend to allow events from any origin (wildcard CORS) on the ingestion route, while maintaining strict isolation and security for all dashboard and project-management APIs.

whatsIncluded.headline: SDK Initialization Pass
whatsIncluded.detail: Hardened the SDK core to ensure tracking always fires, even if the library loads after the page is already interactive (using document.readyState checks).

whatsIncluded.headline: Version Synchronization
whatsIncluded.detail: SDK bumped to v0.2.3 and Root bumped to v0.9.2 to reflect the transition to a truly universal analytics infrastructure.

title: Dashboard Accessibility & Sidebar Polish
summary: This patch resolves critical interactivity issues with the dashboard sidebar and improves overall navigation accessibility with proper hover states and mobile interaction handling.

whatsIncluded.headline: Mobile Sidebar Interactivity Fix
whatsIncluded.detail: Resolved a bug where the sidebar would immediately close on mobile when clicking project links or the sign-out button due to a misplaced event ref.

whatsIncluded.headline: Hover State Feedback
whatsIncluded.detail: Added explicit cursor-pointer feedback to all interactive sidebar elements, ensuring consistent UX across desktop and mobile.

whatsIncluded.headline: Version Synchronization
whatsIncluded.detail: Frontend bumped to v0.6.1 and Root bumped to v0.9.1 to incorporate these critical UX refinements.

title: Dashboard Redesign & Sparklines
summary: Major dashboard layout overhaul. Introduced a two-column structure with a dedicated right-side metrics panel, integrated live sparkline trend graphs, and optimized the chart layout for better data density and readability.

whatsIncluded.headline: Dashboard Layout Redesign
whatsIncluded.detail: New two-column layout: main content area for charts and tables on the left, fixed vertical stats panel on the right. Side-by-side grid for Activity and Browser charts.

whatsIncluded.headline: Live Sparkline Trends
whatsIncluded.detail: Integrated mini-trend charts (sparklines) into all core stat cards, providing instant visual context for Pageviews, Visitors, Sessions, and Events over time.

whatsIncluded.headline: UI Alignment & Polish
whatsIncluded.detail: Perfect height alignment across all panel rows and improved vertical centering for charts. Enhanced responsiveness for browser distribution legend with hover-to-show scrolling.

whatsIncluded.headline: Enhanced Code Blocks
whatsIncluded.detail: Implemented a custom CodeBlock component for /docs with syntax highlighting and copy-to-clipboard support, improving accessibility and developer experience.

whatsIncluded.headline: Version Synchronization
whatsIncluded.detail: Frontend bumped to v0.6.0 and Root bumped to v0.9.0 to coordinate the dashboard milestone release.

title: Homepage & Docs Overhaul + Mobile Fix
summary: Complete rewrite of the homepage and documentation. The homepage is now technically accurate with an SDK code preview, SVG feature cards, and a tech stack section. The docs cover 17 sections derived directly from source code. All mobile responsiveness issues in docs and the changelog modal have been resolved.

whatsIncluded.headline: Homepage Rewrite
whatsIncluded.detail: New SDK code preview panel with syntax highlighting, 2x2 SVG feature cards with per-card gradient accents, tech stack grid with 6 components, and copy that references real code behavior instead of marketing language.

whatsIncluded.headline: Documentation (17 Sections)
whatsIncluded.detail: Full /docs rewrite: Introduction, Quick Start, Installation, Initialization, Tracking Events, Pageview Tracking, Visitor & Session Model, Batching & Delivery, Reliability Guarantees, Configuration Options, API Reference, Backend Ingestion Format, Security & Validation, Local Dev, Production Deployment, FAQ, and Troubleshooting.

whatsIncluded.headline: Docs Mobile Responsiveness
whatsIncluded.detail: Fixed horizontal overflow from code blocks (max-width: 100% on .code-block). Fixed-width flex rows in metadata and API reference cards now stack vertically on mobile with flex-col sm:flex-row.

whatsIncluded.headline: Changelog Modal Mobile Fix
whatsIncluded.detail: Modal height now uses min(600px, 100vh - 2rem). On mobile, the version sidebar becomes a horizontal scrollable pill row. Desktop two-column layout is fully preserved.

title: Cross-Domain Auth Fix & Backend Logging
summary: This release resolves critical cross-domain authentication issues and introduces high-detail diagnostic logging to the backend infrastructure.

whatsIncluded.headline: Cross-Domain Auth Fix
whatsIncluded.detail: Enabled 'SameSite=None' and 'Secure' for authentication cookies in production. This allows session persistence when the frontend (Vercel) and backend (EC2/OCI) are on different domains.

whatsIncluded.headline: Diagnostic Ingestion Logging
whatsIncluded.detail: Implemented a request logger in the main entry point and granular trace logs in the GitHub OAuth flow to provide full visibility into ingestion health and auth success.

whatsIncluded.headline: Version Synchronization
whatsIncluded.detail: Updated backend to v0.5.0 and SDK to v0.2.2 with synchronized metadata across the workspace.

title: Analytics SDK Distribution Fix
summary: This release includes critical distribution fixes for the Vyzora Analytics SDK (v0.2.1), ensuring valid package mapping for both ESM and CommonJS bundlers.

whatsIncluded.headline: SDK Entry Point Fix
whatsIncluded.detail: Corrected package.json paths to match dist/index.js (CJS) and dist/index.mjs (ESM). This resolves 'Module not found' errors in Next.js and other modern build environments.

whatsIncluded.headline: Version Synchronization
whatsIncluded.detail: Synchronized project versioning across the workspace to reflect the latest SDK hardening pass and distribution correction.

title: SDK Production Safety Hardening
summary: A comprehensive production safety pass on the Vyzora Analytics SDK (v0.2.0). This release hardens every layer of the SDK — storage, session, batching, transport, and SPA tracking — to make it bulletproof in real-world environments including Safari private mode.

whatsIncluded.headline: Safe Storage Layer
whatsIncluded.detail: New storage.ts utility wraps all localStorage access in try/catch. Never throws, never crashes — including Safari private mode, storage quota exceeded, and SecurityError scenarios. Visitor and session data degrade gracefully to in-memory fallbacks.

whatsIncluded.headline: Flush Race Condition Fix
whatsIncluded.detail: A flushing lock flag on the Queue class prevents concurrent visibilitychange and pagehide events from triggering duplicate network sends. The lock resets in a try/finally block so a transport error can never cause a permanent queue deadlock.

whatsIncluded.headline: Smart Retry & Full-Path Deduplication
whatsIncluded.detail: Transport retries now limited to 5xx and network errors only — 4xx responses (401, 403, 429) dropped silently. Pageview deduplication upgraded to use full pathname + search, handling query-string navigation while correctly ignoring hash changes.

title: SDK Stabilization & v0.6.1 Release
summary: This release stabilizes the Vyzora Analytics SDK (v0.1.1) with improved batching, interval management, and increased default throughput settings. It also synchronizes versions across the monorepo.

whatsIncluded.headline: SDK Batching Stabilization
whatsIncluded.detail: Increased default batch size to 20 and flush interval to 10s. Fixed duplicate interval creation bugs and confirmed reliable flusing on page visibility changes.

whatsIncluded.headline: System-wide Version Sync
whatsIncluded.detail: Coordinated release across Root (v0.6.1), Frontend (v0.4.2), and SDK (v0.1.1) to ensure full compatibility and consistent documentation.

whatsIncluded.headline: Improved Build Injection
whatsIncluded.detail: Finalized the build-time URL injection mechanism, allowing the SDK endpoint to be configured via environmental variables during production builds.

title: Production Analytics SDK & Polish
summary: This release launches the official Vyzora Analytics SDK (v0.1.0), a lightweight, production-grade tracking library. It also consolidates recent frontend refinements, including the premium preloader and hydration fixes.

whatsIncluded.headline: Official Analytics SDK (v0.1.0)
whatsIncluded.detail: Launched the framework-agnostic Vyzora SDK. Features include batched event ingestion, navigator.sendBeacon reliability, automatic metadata collection, and intelligent 30-minute session management.

whatsIncluded.headline: Premium Preloader Animation
whatsIncluded.detail: Implemented a brand-focused intro sequence with logo fade-ins and a curtain-lift transition to reveal the dashboard.

whatsIncluded.headline: Hydration & Navigation Fixes
whatsIncluded.detail: Resolved Next.js SSR hydration mismatches and fixed a flash-of-content bug by using module-level guards for the preloader.

title: Preloader & Branding Polish
summary: Focused polish release. Introduces a premium homepage preloader animation and fixes a React hydration mismatch, alongside branding and metadata refinements across the frontend.

whatsIncluded.headline: Executive-Style Preloader Animation
whatsIncluded.detail: Built a multi-phase homepage preloader: logo and brand name fade in, a gradient indigo-to-purple progress bar fills across the bottom, then the entire screen slides upward like a curtain lift to reveal the application.

whatsIncluded.headline: Smart Preloader Guard
whatsIncluded.detail: The preloader activates only on the homepage and only once per real browser page load. A module-level flag prevents it from re-triggering on client-side navigation, eliminating the flash-of-content bug.

whatsIncluded.headline: Hydration Error Fix
whatsIncluded.detail: Resolved a Next.js SSR hydration mismatch caused by accessing `window` during the synchronous render phase. The preloader now activates via `useEffect`, ensuring server and client render identical initial HTML.

whatsIncluded.headline: Branding & Metadata Refinements
whatsIncluded.detail: Updated browser tab title, meta description, favicon (now SVG), and Footer copy to professional engineering-focused language.

title: Advanced Analytics & Aggregation
summary: This milestone adds a complete behavioral analytics layer. Vyzora now transforms raw events into actionable insights with time-series charts, session exploration, and geographic/browser distribution.

whatsIncluded.headline: Real-time Aggregation Engine
whatsIncluded.detail: Engineered high-performance SQL aggregation logic for daily time-series, top-performing pages, and event frequency analysis.

whatsIncluded.headline: Full Analytics Dashboard
whatsIncluded.detail: Launched a comprehensive dashboard featuring Recharts-powered time-series, interactive tables, and donut charts for browser distribution.

whatsIncluded.headline: Session Explorer UI
whatsIncluded.detail: Introduced a deep-dive session table with automatic duration calculation and event density tracking per user session.

whatsIncluded.headline: Optimized Ownership Guard
whatsIncluded.detail: All analytics queries are now protected by a hardened ownership validation layer ensuring strict data privacy across projects.

title: Auth, Projects & Analytics Core
summary: This release establishes Vyzora as a functional end-to-end analytics platform. It introduces secure GitHub authentication, self-serve project management, and a high-performance DB-backed metrics pipeline.

whatsIncluded.headline: GitHub OAuth & Secure Sessions
whatsIncluded.detail: Implemented professional-grade authentication using GitHub OAuth and HttpOnly JWT cookies for tamper-proof session management.

whatsIncluded.headline: Project Management & Rate Limiting
whatsIncluded.detail: Users can now create, list, and delete projects directly from the dashboard, protected by per-user ceilings and hourly rate limits.

whatsIncluded.headline: Optimized Analytics Pipeline
whatsIncluded.detail: Replaced in-memory data processing with raw SQL aggregation and composite indexing, enabling fast sub-second metrics in the dashboard.

whatsIncluded.headline: Fully Wired Dashboard
whatsIncluded.detail: The dashboard is now 100% connected to the backend, displaying real-time events, unique visitors, and session counts from the database.

title: SaaS Landing Page & Docs
summary: Transformed Vyzora from a backend-only engine into a complete SaaS product with a premium landing page, reactive documentation, and a cohesive design system.

whatsIncluded.headline: Responsive SaaS Landing Page
whatsIncluded.detail: Implemented a high-conversion hero section, interactive feature cards, and architectural pipeline visualization. Powered by a custom particle background system.

whatsIncluded.headline: Dynamic Sticky Navbar
whatsIncluded.detail: Built a scroll-reactive navigation system that transitions from floating to fixed with backdrop-blur, subtle borders, and shadow depth.

whatsIncluded.headline: Developer Documentation System
whatsIncluded.detail: Created a dedicated docs layout with a sticky sidebar, anchor-based navigation, and comprehensive guides for SDK usage and API reference.

whatsIncluded.headline: Design System & Utilities
whatsIncluded.detail: Established a dark-theme design system using Tailwind 4, featuring indigo/purple gradients, glassmorphism, and responsive container layouts.

title: Core Ingestion Layer
summary: Built the full event ingestion pipeline from SDK to database. Every event is validated, batched, and written to Supabase Postgres with composite indexing for fast time-range queries.

whatsIncluded.headline: PostgreSQL schema — User, Project, Event
whatsIncluded.detail: Three relational models with UUID primary keys, VarChar constraints, and cascade deletes. Composite index on (projectId, createdAt) for time-range analytics.

whatsIncluded.headline: Secure API key generation
whatsIncluded.detail: 64-character hex keys generated with crypto.randomBytes(32). Each project gets a unique key — invalid keys are rejected before hitting the database.

whatsIncluded.headline: Bulk ingest with createMany
whatsIncluded.detail: Events are batched and written in a single Prisma createMany call. A 1mb body guard prevents oversized payloads.

whatsIncluded.headline: Strict Zod validation
whatsIncluded.detail: Every field length matches the DB schema: sessionId/visitorId ≤128, eventType ≤64, path ≤512. Validation errors return structured 400 responses.

title: Project Scaffold
summary: Initialised the Vyzora monorepo with separate workspaces for the backend API, Next.js dashboard, and TypeScript runtime SDK. Established the base configuration for all three packages.

whatsIncluded.headline: Backend scaffold — Express + Prisma
whatsIncluded.detail: Node.js 18 + TypeScript server with route, controller, service, and config layers. Prisma configured for PostgreSQL with a prisma.config.ts entry point.

whatsIncluded.headline: Frontend scaffold — Next.js 15
whatsIncluded.detail: App Router with dark theme Tailwind config, route stubs for /dashboard and /login, and globals.css design tokens.

whatsIncluded.headline: Runtime SDK scaffold
whatsIncluded.detail: TypeScript package with tsup for ESM + CJS dual output. Modules: tracker.ts, session.ts, batch.ts.

whatsIncluded.headline: Documentation stubs
whatsIncluded.detail: architecture.md, api-spec.md, and sdk-design.md in the /docs workspace.
